steps.title: Identify the opening em-dash
steps.content: The em-dash after postmodern dance opens a non-restrictive relative clause (which demystifies traditional choreography by foregrounding...) that provides additional information about what the dance does.

steps.title: Close the em-dash pair
steps.content:
Identify the structure: "the principles of postmodern dance—which demystifies...movement that ballet and other classical dance forms obscure (such as clumsiness, weightiness, resistance, and [BLANK] to video." The parenthetical opened by an em dash must be closed by a matching em dash before "to video."
  • Choice A — gravity),: "...resistance, and gravity), to video." — comma after the closing parenthesis cannot close an em-dash-opened parenthetical; creates a mismatched bracket. ✗
  • Choice B — gravity);: "...resistance, and gravity); to video." — semicolon cannot close an em-dash parenthetical and would require an IC to follow. ✗
  • Choice C — gravity)—: "...resistance, and gravity)—to video." — em dash matches the opening em dash, correctly closing the parenthetical; the sentence then continues with "to video." ✓
  • Choice D — gravity): "...resistance, and gravity) to video." — no closing punctuation leaves the em-dash-opened parenthetical unclosed; "to video" floats without proper attachment. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
